title: Finite-Difference MI Gradient

content.hypothesis: True MI gradient (via finite differences) can optimize beta/gamma
content.method: Compute MI(beta±ε, gamma±ε), use finite differences to estimate gradient, apply gradient ascent
content.result: Improves MI in 4/5 starting points, but converges to 5 DIFFERENT local maxima. No global attractor. Multiple basins confirmed
content.root_cause: MI landscape has multiple local maxima. Violates Principle II (requires external MI measurement, not intrinsic to computation)

constraints_implied: Finite-diff MI gradient violates Principle II (external measurement, not self-generated)
Beta/gamma MI landscape has multiple local maxima (no unique optimum)
